name: Architext 1.7.3 Routing Correctness + Diagram Fidelity
summary: Patch release hardening the deterministic Rust router and the diagram renderers. The model router gains a permanent forbidden-artifact gate (zero doglegs, Z-staircases, non-orthogonal segments, unrouted edges, min-stems, or channel overlaps), shape-preserving channel separation, and crossing reduction (28 -> 21 on the FlowForge corpus; the model is never worse than the engine at a fraction of its bend cost). Sequence diagrams follow the UML standard; the Sequence-mode flow-selection papercut and an overline-tracking drift are fixed.
updateSource: Merged to develop via PR #238 (CI green: verify-rust + package). NOT yet shipped to npm/main — release-candidate pending the develop -> main ceremony. Validation at merge: routing 525 lib tests, viewer 127 tests, forbidden-artifact corpus gate 1/1, corpus_fitness perf ratchet 1/1, native validator pass. Crossings 21 == engine 21 with beta 174 vs engine 1338.

required.title: Channel separation + permanent forbidden-artifact gate
required.summary: Shape-preserving channel separation removes route overlaps (8 -> 0) and pushes close-parallel channel buffers to within an arrowhead (tight 19 -> 2, the residual accept-with-log). A permanent gate (audit_model binary + an in-repo corpus test) holds the model router to zero forbidden artifacts: doglegs, Z/staircases, non-orthogonal segments, unrouted edges, min-stems, and channel overlaps. Crossings fall 28 -> 21, including an orientation-independent arch-mount nesting fix that took the model-inference / agent-turn-flow bundle to zero.

required.title: Dense-flow routing performance
required.summary: In model-review mode the engine's separate_close_parallel_routes pass is skipped, and channel separation runs once on the final routing instead of per optimizer trial. Dense flows that took minutes now route in milliseconds.

required.title: Sequence diagrams follow the UML standard
required.summary: Return messages are inferred by call/return pairing (untagged corpus returns now render dashed with an open arrowhead; synchronous calls stay solid with a filled head) and close their activation bars. Combined fragments render a SOLID border (dashing is reserved for return/async messages and lifelines), a pentagon operator tab, no adjacent-fragment overlap, and a right-aligned guard clear of the first message label.

required.title: Sequence-mode flow preservation + overline tracking
required.summary: Switching into Sequence mode no longer resets the flow dropdown to the first flow (it kept the selection's view but discarded the flow). The design-fidelity audit converged three overline labels to var(--overline-tracking) per DESIGN.md item 2; the audit was otherwise clean (role color single-sourced via --c4-*, state never a role hue, no rogue fonts).

required.title: Clear clippy -D warnings on the routing crate
required.summary: The wasm viewer clippy CI step (which lints architext-routing as a dependency under -D warnings) was red on lint debt accumulated across the routing-overhaul commits, uncaught because the branch had no prior PR/CI and cargo test does not run clippy. Cleared (unused_mut, map_or, an OR-pattern range, and two provably-dead route-cache assignments) with routing output byte-identical.

deferred.title: Collapse the greedy mount-order passes into a direct track assignment
deferred.summary: Deferred after a measured prototype disproved the hypothesis (Rule 17): a global iterative-barycenter track assignment lands on the SAME 21 crossings the greedy finds (the residual is a structural optimum, not order-fixable), and the assignment ALONE (greedy disabled) gives 92 crossings, 4x worse. The greedy local search's per-candidate crossing evaluation is irreducible; a direct assignment merely re-derives it. Deferral reason: the refactor would not simplify or improve quality, so the current greedy stands.

workstreams.name: Deterministic router correctness + performance
workstreams.summary: Bring the model router to a gated, correct, performant state: channel separation, the forbidden-artifact gate, crossing reduction, and dense-flow speed — verified against the FlowForge corpus.

workstreams.name: Sequence + design fidelity
workstreams.summary: Sequence diagrams to the UML standard and a design-language fidelity pass over the viewer chrome, plus the Sequence-mode flow-selection papercut.

evidence.label: PR #238 router correctness + sequence/design fidelity (merged to develop)
